data.title: This AI-Enabled Manufacturer's Business Is Booming. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Proto Labs, a company that prototypes and manufactures difficult to make parts, reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings with the help of artificial intelligence and a new strategy.

The advanced manufacturing technology company on Friday reported first-quarter earnings per share of 54 cents on sales of $139.4 million, up 10.4% year over year. Wall Street was looking for about 40 cents in EPS and sales of $135 million, according to FactSet.

For the second quarter, Proto Labs projects 54 cents in EPS from sales of $144 million. Wall Street currently projects 47 cents in EPS and $144 million in sales.

It looks like a solid quarter. The stock gyrated wildly in response. Proto Labs stock was up almost 6% in premarket trading, above $68, but the gains faded. Shares traded as low as $57.67 before closing at $65.17, up 0.6%. The S&P 500 rose 0.3% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.3%.

Starting points might help explain the volatility. Coming into Friday's session, Proto Labs stock was up 28% this year and up 81% over the past 12 months. Those gains left shares trading for about 35 times earnings expected over the coming 12 months, up from 25 times a year ago.

Proto Labs excels at rapid prototyping and building custom parts for its customers using automated CNC machines, injection molds, and 3D printers. It is moving beyond prototyping with the help of AI, which can connect machines, assist with design, and make ordering easier for customers.

Proto Labs does a significant amount of business with medical technology, aerospace, and transportation firms.

"These results reflect strong demand for Protolabs' digital manufacturing services, and disciplined execution across the business," said CEO Suresh Krishna. "We're making continued progress across our strategic pillars while strengthening the organization to support long--term growth and scalability."

The company's so-called pillars are: enhancing the customer experience, serving them from prototyping to production; accelerating innovation with the help of AI; growing production; and improving efficiency.

"Each of our four strategic pillars reinforces the others, and collectively, they drive a more customer-centric, innovative, efficient, and scalable Protolabs," said Krishna in February.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: SPAC Blueport Acquisition, SingAuto Agree to $1.2B Merger
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Blueport Acquisition, a special purpose acquisition company, entered into a business combination agreement with SingAuto, a Singapore-based firm that provides technology solutions for electric vehicles, in a $1.2 billion deal.

The companies agreed to form a new holding company, which will be listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market. The company is expected to be led by SingAuto Chief Executive Yuqiang Lu.

SingAuto shareholders will receive about 120 million shares of the combined public company, valued at $10 each a share.

"We believe SingAuto is a uniquely compelling company with green cold-chain logistics technology solutions for smart commercial electric vehicles that will benefit from being a public company," Blueport Chief Executive William Rosenstadt said.

The deal has been approved by the boards of both companies and is expected to close by the end of the year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: TSX erases weekly gain as energy and metal mining shares fall
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSX ends down 0.2% at 33,891.18
  • Energy declines 1.1% as oil settles nearly 3% lower
  • Materials group loses 1.1% as gold dips
  • Badger Infrastructure jumps 19.3% on Q1 revenue beat

By Tharuniyaa Lakshmi and Fergal Smith

Canada's main stock index dipped on Friday, leaving it barely changed for the week, as losses for resource shares offset gains for technology, and investors weighed prospects of diplomatic progress in the Iran war.

The Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX Composite Index ended down 73.15 points, or 0.2%, at 33,891.18. For the week, the index inched 0.04% lower, while it has advanced 6.9% since the start of the year.

Tehran has submitted its latest proposal for negotiations with the United States, Iranian state media and a Pakistani official said, a move that could offer hope for breaking a deadlock in efforts to end the Iran war.

"There’s going to be a lot of uncertainty until the tensions kind of die down," said Webull Canada CEO Michael Constantino.

The conflict has spurred inventory building in Canada's manufacturing sector, leading to the sector expanding in April at its fastest pace in nearly four years.

Energy fell 1.1% as oil gave back some recent gains. U.S. crude oil futures settled nearly 3% lower at $101.94 a barrel.

Imperial Oil missed analysts' estimates for first-quarter profit, as weaker crude realizations and unplanned outages at its facilities reduced refinery throughput. The oil producers' shares fell 4%.

The materials group , which includes metal mining shares, lost 1.1% as the price of gold edged lower.

Three of the 10 major sectors ended higher, including technology . It rose 1.9%, with shares of e-commerce company Shopify Inc up 5.2%.

Badger Infrastructure Solutions Ltd was a standout. Its shares jumped 19.3% after the excavating firm reported first-quarter revenue that beat estimates.

data.title: Veradermics Raises $30 Million in Private Pre-Funded Warrant Deal With Suvretta Affiliates
data.description:
Veradermics entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement for a concurrent private placement with entities affiliated with Suvretta Capital. The company sold pre-funded warrants to purchase 300,000 shares at $99.99999 per warrant, with a $0.00001 exercise price, generating approximately $30 million in gross proceeds. The deal closed alongside Veradermics' public offering of 3,843,790 shares at $100.00 per share, with a 30-day option for 576,568 additional shares. The warrants feature a 9.99% beneficial ownership cap, adjustable up to 19.99% with notice.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for private placement of pre-funded warrants
  • Counterparty: Suvretta Capital affiliates
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 29 2026 / May 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until fully exercised
  • Reason: Raise capital alongside public offering

Original SEC Filing: Veradermics, Inc [ MANE ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Vanda Pharmaceuticals US Availability Of Nereus For People With Motion Sickness
data.description:
Vanda Pharmaceuticals Inc :

data.title: Global Industrial Co expected to post earnings of 41 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Global Industrial Co , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Port Washington New York-based company is expected to report a 7.2% increase in revenue to $344.13 million from $321 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Global Industrial Co is for earnings of 41 cents per share.

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "hold".

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Global Industrial Co is $40.00, about 20.8% above its last closing price of $33.11

data.title: DPM Metals Inc expected to post earnings of 90 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• DPM Metals Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Toronto Ontario-based company is expected to report a 55.0% increase in revenue to $223.41 million from $144.1 million a year ago, according to the estimate from one analyst, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for DPM Metals Inc is for earnings of 90 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 11 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 5.2% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for DPM Metals Inc is C$63.50, about 39.2% above its last closing price of C$45.61

data.description:
Q1 2026 saw stable net income and NOI, but FFO declined 7.6% year-over-year amid higher vacancies and FX headwinds. Canadian and U.S. AMR rose, though occupancy fell in both regions. A major CAD 1B portfolio acquisition is progressing, with closing expected in H2 2026.

data.title: InspireMD Withdraws Guidance, Recalls CGuard Prime
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

InspireMD is voluntarily recalling its CGuard Prime 135 cm carotid stent delivery system after determining that the system did not meet performance expectations.

The medical technology company said Friday that the voluntary recall came after the stent system for the prevention of stroke did not meet expectations during carotid artery stenting procedures.

The company is implementing design improvements to the CGuard Prime stent delivery system and expects approval from the Food and Drug Administration by the first half of 2027, the company said.

The company is withdrawing its full-year revenue guidance.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com
